text.title: Outcome Roadmap Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for authoring, maintaining, and publishing outcome-based roadmaps, including agent-specific gotchas.

rule.statement: Pair every outcome with a falsifying signal: "If activation drops below X by week 6, kill this bet." Without a kill signal, the outcome cannot be closed — teams keep pursuing it indefinitely.
rule.rationale: Kill signals force honesty in retrospectives. An outcome without one will always find a reason to continue, burning resources that should pivot.

rule.statement: Tie every outcome to an existing analytics dashboard before publishing. If you cannot link a chart URL, the outcome is not yet real enough to plan around.
rule.rationale: Outcomes anchored to real data prevent the common failure of setting targets without knowing the baseline. Agents will hallucinate plausible baselines — inject real data as context.

rule.statement: Maintain two publishing artefacts: an internal roadmap (outcomes + bets + targets) and an external/customer roadmap (themes and benefits only, no metric targets). Sync via script, not copy-paste.
rule.rationale: Sharing metric targets externally creates contractual expectations. Customers interpret "reduce churn to 5%" as a promise; "improve retention" keeps strategic flexibility while communicating direction.

rule.statement: Keep outcomes immutable for the entire quarter. Only the candidate solutions list mutates as experiments resolve. Rewrite outcomes only at quarter boundaries.
rule.rationale: Mid-quarter outcome changes invalidate all in-flight experiments and destroy the team's ability to measure progress. Solutions can pivot freely; the goal cannot.

rule.statement: Update cadence: review the solution list monthly, rewrite outcomes quarterly. Do not rewrite the whole document every sprint.
rule.rationale: Over-frequent rewrites destroy the historical signal that makes retrospectives useful. Diffs between quarters are the primary learning artefact.

rule.statement: When using an LLM to generate outcome roadmaps: require a metric + delta + verb in every outcome row, pull real baselines from analytics before prompting, clamp candidate solution lists to 6, and mark the not-doing field as required.
rule.rationale: LLMs restate features as outcomes ("ship onboarding redesign" becomes "improve onboarding"), hallucinate numeric baselines, balloon solution lists, and silently drop the not-doing column because it produces no generative content.

rule.statement: Never let an agent publish the external roadmap directly. Human checkpoint mandatory — outcome targets can be commitments; competitive and NDA implications require human review.
rule.rationale: An LLM cannot assess business risk, NDAs, or competitive signalling context. These are human judgment calls.
